HRES 311 IH
102d CONGRESS
1st Session
H. RES. 311
To amend the Rules of the House of Representatives to provide for the election
of the chairmen and ranking minority members of the standing committees,
and for other purposes.
IN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES
November 26, 1991
Mr. MCCURDY (for himself, Mr. STENHOLM, Mr. SABO, Mr. ANDREWS of Texas,
and Mr. GLICKMAN) submitted the following resolution; which was referred to
the Committee on Rules
RESOLUTION
To amend the Rules of the House of Representatives to provide for the election
of the chairmen and ranking minority members of the standing committees,
and for other purposes.
  Resolved,
SECTION 1. APPOINTMENT OF STANDING COMMITTEE CHAIRMEN AND RANKING MINORITY
MEMBERS.
  (a) RULES CHANGE- Clause 6(c) of rule X of the Rules of the House of
  Representatives is amended to read as follows:
  `(c)(1) The chairmen and the ranking minority member of the standing
  committees shall be elected as follows:
  `(A) The Speaker shall nominate a Member of a standing committee for
  election by the majority party caucus as the chairman of such committee
  and the minority leader shall nominate a Member of a standing committee
  for election by the minority party caucus as the ranking minority member of
  such committee. If either caucus does not elect a Member nominated by the
  Speaker or the ranking minority member, the chairman or ranking minority
  member shall be elected in accordance with subparagraph (B).
  `(B) The House shall elect a chairman of a standing committee from
  nominations of Members of such committee submitted by the majority party
  caucus. The House shall elect a ranking minority member of a standing
  committee from nominations of Members of such committee submitted by the
  minority party caucus.
  `(2) No Member may serve as the chairman or ranking minority member of a
  standing committee for more than 4 Congresses in any period of 6 successive
  Congresses (disregarding for this purpose any service performed as chairman
  or ranking minority member of such committee for less than a full session
  in any Congress or before the One Hundred Second Congress).
  `(3) In the temporary absence of the chairman of a standing committee, the
  Member next in rank in the order named in the election of the committee,
  and so on, as often as the case shall happen, shall act as chairman. In the
  case of a permanent vacancy, the chairman shall be elected in accordance
  with paragraph (1).'.
  (b) CONFORMING AMENDMENT- The heading for clause 6 of rule X of the Rules
  of the House of Representatives is amended by striking out `Chairmen'
  and inserting in lieu thereof `Election and Terms of Chairmen and Ranking
  Minority Members'.
SEC. 2. EFFECTIVE DATE.
  The amendment made by section 1 shall take effect immediately before noon
  January 3, 1993.
